note: Keys must exactly match FG_SMARTS keys in constants/fg_smarts.py (plus 'Steroid' which uses Python detection). Rebuild db/fg_residue_table.csv after any key change.

Carboxylic acid.description: RC(=O)OH group. Strong H-bond donor + acceptor; ionisable (pKa ~4.5). Engages Asp/Glu/Arg/Lys in active sites via salt bridge or direct coordination.

Ester.description: Acyclic RC(=O)OR' group (ring esters are captured by 'Lactone'). H-bond acceptor only; metabolically labile. Hydrolysed by esterases and lipases; prodrug strategy.

Amide.description: RC(=O)NHR' group. Resonance-stabilised; H-bond donor + acceptor. Backbone motif of peptide substrates; preferred pharmacophore in protease/kinase inhibitors.

Lactone.description: Cyclic ester (-C(=O)-O- in ring). Includes gamma-lactones (5-membered), delta-lactones (6-membered), and aromatic lactones (coumarins). Superset of Coumarin and Macrolide. Hydrolysed by lactonases and esterases; HDAC inhibitors often contain a hydroxamic acid or lactone zinc-binding group; some nuclear receptor ligands are lactones.

Ketone.description: RC(=O)R' group (both neighbors are C). Pure H-bond acceptor. Targeted by oxidoreductases; electrophilic warhead in activated ketone inhibitors (e.g., alpha-keto amide warhead in NS3A/4A protease inhibitors). Cyclic ketones in HDAC inhibitors (romidepsin, trapoxin, spiruchostatin) coordinate zinc at the active site as a zinc-binding group in class I/II HDAC.

Aldehyde.description: RCH=O group. Reactive electrophile; forms Schiff bases with Lys. Substrate for ALDH/ADH; found in some natural product warheads.

Hydroxyl.description: Aliphatic -OH group (excludes phenols and acid/ester OH). H-bond donor + acceptor. Engaged by Ser/Thr/Tyr kinases; common in sugar and nucleotide ligands.

Phenol.description: ArOH group. Stronger H-bond donor than aliphatic OH (pKa ~10); can ionise. Key pharmacophore in oestrogen receptor ligands, COX inhibitors, and COMT substrates.

Catechol.description: Ortho-dihydroxybenzene motif. Strong metal chelator (Fe3+, Cu2+, Zn2+); COMT substrate. Present in quercetin, EGCG, catecholamines. Susceptible to oxidation.

Methylenedioxy.description: -OCH2O- bridge across two adjacent aromatic carbons (1,3-benzodioxole motif). Present in piperine, safrole, methylenedioxymethamphetamine. The methylene C is a site for CYP450 carbene intermediate formation; mechanism-based inhibitor of CYP450 and MAO.

Ether.description: C-O-C group (not carbonyl, not OH, not ester oxygen). Weak H-bond acceptor. Metabolised by CYP450 O-dealkylation; common in drug scaffolds for improving bioavailability.

Primary amine.description: RNH2 group (excludes amide, sulfonamide, aromatic N). Ionisable (pKa ~10); strong H-bond donor. Substrate for MAO and amine oxidases; engages Asp/Glu by salt bridge.

Secondary amine.description: R2NH group (excludes amide, sulfonamide, aromatic N). H-bond donor + acceptor. Common in GPCR ligands; substrate for MAO-B.

Tertiary amine.description: R3N group (excludes imines, amides, sulfonamides, aromatic N). Non-ionisable in physiological range; H-bond acceptor only. Found in muscarinic/nicotinic ligands.

Triazole.description: Five-membered aromatic ring with three N atoms (1,2,4-triazole n-c-n-c-n pattern). Covers N-H and N-substituted triazoles. Key pharmacophore in triazole antifungals (fluconazole, voriconazole, itraconazole, posaconazole) that coordinate fungal CYP51 heme iron via the free triazole N, inhibiting lanosterol 14α-demethylase.

Thiazole.description: 5-membered aromatic S,N-heterocycle (1,3-thiazole). Ritonavir-class CYP3A4 inhibitor scaffold; thiazole N coordinates heme Fe(III). Also in meloxicam (COX), sulfathiazole.

Benzimidazole.description: Benzo[d]imidazole: benzene fused to imidazole. PPI scaffold (omeprazole), tubulin binders (mebendazole), kinase inhibitors. Contains imidazole substructure (detected separately by Imidazole pattern). No standalone target annotation — interactions captured via Imidazole FG votes.

Imidazole.description: Five-membered aromatic heterocycle with N1 and N3. Mimics histidine; coordinates metal ions (Fe, Zn, Cu). Key motif in CYP450 inhibitors (azole antifungals) and histamine receptor ligands.

Indole.description: Bicyclic aromatic scaffold: pyrrole ring fused to benzene. Defining pharmacophore of tryptamine alkaloids (serotonin, melatonin, psilocybin, vincristine). Indole NH is an H-bond donor; the pi-system engages Trp/Phe residues. Partially overlaps Phenyl ring (benzene portion still detected).

Purine.description: Fused 5+6 bicyclic N-heterocycle (imidazole fused to pyrimidine). 4 N atoms; the SMARTS does not require N-H and therefore matches all N-substituted and N-H purines. Scaffold of adenine, guanine, hypoxanthine, inosine, caffeine, theophylline, and most nucleoside analog antivirals (acyclovir, tenofovir). Superset of Xanthine. Partially overlaps Imidazole (5-membered ring portion) and Xanthine (methylxanthine subset).

Xanthine.description: 2,6-Dioxopurine scaffold: purine with C=O at positions 2 and 6. Subset of Purine. Scaffold of xanthine, caffeine (1,3,7-trimethyl), theophylline (1,3-dimethyl), and theobromine (3,7-dimethyl). The SMARTS requires two aromatic C=O groups in the 6-membered ring, uniquely distinguishing this from adenine/guanine purines. Caffeine and theophylline act as adenosine receptor antagonists and PDE inhibitors; theophylline is a classic bronchodilator.

Nitrile.description: RC≡N group. Electrophilic carbon; forms reversible covalent adduct with Cys/Ser. Used as warhead in covalent protease inhibitors (e.g. nirmatrelvir). Also present in nitrile-containing nuclear receptor ligands (e.g. enzalutamide for AR, some thyroid hormone receptor agonists).

Nitro.description: -NO2 group. Electron withdrawing; bioreduced to nitroso/hydroxylamine/amine by nitroreductases and CYP450. Found in some antibiotics (metronidazole) and kinase inhibitors.

Benzamidine.description: Amidino group (-C(=NH)NH2) attached to an aromatic ring. Arginine-mimicking pharmacophore; the positively-charged amidinium (pKa ~11.6) fills the Asp/Glu-rich S1 specificity pocket of serine proteases (thrombin, trypsin, factor Xa, tPA, urokinase). Present in benzamidine itself, anticoagulants (dabigatran precursor), and antiparasitic drugs. The two NH groups form bidentate H-bonds with Asp189 (trypsin numbering).

Thiol.description: R-SH group. Strong nucleophile; coordinates metal ions (Zn2+, Fe, Cu). Undergoes disulfide exchange (Cys active sites). Key in cysteine proteases, thioredoxins, metalloenzymes.

Sulfonamide.description: RS(=O)2NR'2 group. Strong H-bond donor + acceptor. Inhibits carbonic anhydrase by direct zinc coordination via NH. Broad antimicrobial scaffold. Note: acylsulfonamide (C(=O)-NH-SO2) is captured by the separate 'Acylsulfonamide' FG to avoid conflating the two pharmacophores.

Methylsulfone.description: Methyl sulfone group (-SO2CH3) attached to an aromatic ring. Signature COX-2 selectivity pharmacophore: the para-methylsulfonyl group of celecoxib, rofecoxib, and valdecoxib inserts into a hydrophilic side-pocket formed by Arg513/Phe518 in COX-2. This pocket is blocked in COX-1 (Val523 replaces a smaller residue). Not present in classical NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen) or the Sulfonamide class of coxibs — distinct from SO2NH2.

Hydroxamate.description: RC(=O)NHOH group. Bidentate zinc-chelating warhead: both the C=O and N-OH oxygens coordinate the catalytic Zn2+ in HDAC and MMP active sites. Signature pharmacophore of FDA-approved HDAC inhibitors vorinostat (SAHA), belinostat, panobinostat, and pracinostat. Also present in matrix metalloproteinase inhibitors (batimastat, marimastat). Structurally distinct from Amide (no OH) and Ester (no NH).

Acylsulfonamide.description: RC(=O)-NH-S(=O)2-R' group. Distinguishes the cryptophycin / epothilone-class tubulin-binding macrolide pharmacophore from classical sulfonamides. The acyl-NH bridges C=O and SO2, forming a rigid template that positions the macrolide correctly for tubulin beta-subunit binding. Also a pharmacophore in some beta-lactamase inhibitors. Distinct from Sulfonamide (no C=O before N) and Amide (no S after N). Mechanistic role: scaffold geometry warhead for tubulin beta-subunit binding in macrolide natural products (cryptophycin, epothilone).

Phenyl ring.description: Unsubstituted or substituted benzene ring. Pi-stacking with Phe/Tyr/Trp; hydrophobic contacts. Ubiquitous in kinase inhibitors, GPCRs ligands, and nuclear receptor agonists/antagonists.

Coumarin.description: 2H-1-Benzopyran-2-one: benzene ring fused to a 6-membered aromatic lactone with the C=O at C2. Key scaffold in anticoagulants (warfarin), MAO inhibitors (umbelliferone), and CYP450-metabolised phytochemicals. Subset of Lactone; overlaps Phenyl ring (benzene portion).

Chromone.description: 4H-1-Benzopyran-4-one: benzene ring fused to a 6-membered ring with C=O at C4 and ring O at C1. Backbone of flavone, flavonol, and isoflavone scaffolds (quercetin, kaempferol, genistein). NOT a lactone (C=O is a ring ketone; ring O is an ether). Target class: COX, kinase, estrogen receptor, tyrosine kinase.

Steroid.description: Tetracyclic 6-6-6-5 fused ring scaffold (cyclopentanophenanthrene). A-B-C rings are six-membered, D ring is five-membered, all carbon. Detected via Python function _detect_steroid_core (SMARTS not used — see utils/fg_detector.py). Present in testosterone, cholesterol, cortisol, estradiol, bile acids.

Halogen.description: C-X bond (F, Cl, Br, I). F: metabolic block, lipophilicity tuning; Cl/Br: halogen bond donor to carbonyl O (backbone C=O of protein); I: bulky, used in X-ray structure work. Halogens are common in nuclear receptor ligands (thyroid hormone receptor: T3/T4 have iodine; fluorinated retinoids; chlorinated estrogens) and CYP450 substrates.

Epoxide.description: Three-membered ring with O. Highly reactive electrophile; alkylates Lys/Cys/His in active sites. Natural product warhead (terpenoids, mitomycin). Detoxified by epoxide hydrolase.

Endoperoxide.description: O-O bond within a ring (cyclic peroxide). Pharmacophore of artemisinin-class antimalarials; the endoperoxide bridge reacts with heme Fe(II) to generate carbon-centred radicals that alkylate parasite proteins. Distinct from acyclic organic peroxides and from the epoxide pattern.

α,β-unsat. carbonyl.description: Michael acceptor motif C=C-C=O. Undergoes 1,4-addition with soft nucleophiles (Cys-SH, Lys-NH2). Covalent warhead in natural products (curcumin, parthenolide, withaferin A) and designed covalent inhibitors. Enone-containing HDAC inhibitors (tubastatin analogs, withanolides) act via Michael addition at the Cys151/Cys273/Cys288 residues of some HDAC isoforms.

Macrolide.description: Lactone in a macrocyclic ring of 12 or more atoms. Subset of Lactone; excludes all rings up to 11-membered. Pharmacophore class of erythromycin (14-membered, ribosome 50S), rapamycin/sirolimus (31-membered, mTOR complex), tacrolimus/FK506 (23-membered, calcineurin via FKBP12). Macrolides bind ribosomes (antibacterial), mTOR complex (immunosuppressant/anticancer), calcineurin (immunosuppressant), and tubulin (cryptophycin/epothilone-class microtubule destabilisers).

Morpholine.description: Saturated 1,4-oxazinane (O and N at 1,4). Signature hinge-binding group of ATP-competitive PI3K/mTOR kinase inhibitors (AZD8055, vistusertib, dactolisib); the ring O H-bonds the hinge residue (Val2240 in mTOR). Scaffold marker only - mTOR voting handled by the morpholino-diazine conditional rule (morpholine alone is promiscuous).

Pyrimidine.description: 1,3-Diazine aromatic ring. Core heteroarene of ATP-competitive PI3K/mTOR inhibitors (paired with morpholine) and many kinase scaffolds. Scaffold marker only (too promiscuous to vote); mTOR voting gated on Morpholine + (Pyrimidine|Triazine) combo.

Triazine.description: 1,3,5-Triazine aromatic ring. Hinge-anchoring heteroarene of the morpholino-triazine PI3K/mTOR class (gedatolisib, PF-04691502). Scaffold marker only; voting via morpholino-diazine conditional rule.

Anthraquinone.description: 9,10-anthraquinone (planar tricyclic quinone). DNA-intercalating pharmacophore of anthracycline topoisomerase-II poisons (doxorubicin, daunorubicin, epirubicin, idarubicin) and mitoxantrone; stabilises the Topo-II-DNA cleavage complex.
